sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: I'm spending way too many actions on this level. Let me check the current state and try a different approach. Maybe I should try pressing SPACE from the current position to see if it does something useful.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

print("Green/White:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ('N', 'W'):
        print(f"  {n['color']}: boundary={n['boundary']}, pixels={n['pixels']}")

print("\nBoxes:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ('c', 'G') and n['pixels'] == 12:
        print(f"  {n['color']}: boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check adjacency
adj = seg['adjacency_list']
print("\nAdjacency:")
for a in adj:
    i, j = a
    ci = next((n['color'] for n in nodes if n['id'] == i), '?')
    cj = next((n['color'] for n in nodes if n['id'] == j), '?')
    print(f"  {ci}({i}) - {cj}({j})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! The green (N) is adjacent to the box c(3) at rows 24-27, cols 44-47. The green is at rows 28-31, cols 45-47. Wait, the green's boundary is [[28, 45], [28, 47], [31, 47], [31, 45]], so rows 28-31, cols 45-47. The box c(3) is at rows 24-27, cols 44-47.
The green is BELOW the box (green's top row 28 is just below box's bottom row 27). They share cols 45-47. So the green is adjacent to the box from BELOW.
The white tail W(6) is also adjacent to the box c(3). The tail is at rows 28-31, cols 40-44. The tail's right edge (col 44) is adjacent to the box's left edge (col 44)... wait, the box is at cols 44-47 and the tail is at cols 40-44. So the tail's right edge is at col 44, which is the box's left edge. So the tail is adjacent to the box from the LEFT.
The green is adjacent to the box from BELOW. Earlier, I observed that the box turns G when the green is adjacent from the RIGHT. Let me try pressing SPACE now that the green is adjacent from BELOW to see if that also works.
Actually, let me reconsider. Maybe the goal isn't to turn the boxes G. Maybe the goal is something else entirely, and I've been pursuing the wrong objective.
Let me try pressing SPACE now and see what happens.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Green is adjacent to box from BELOW. Try SPACE.

action(['SPACE'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
